126916.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue is significant, a referendum can be a fair way to make a decision. This engages people in local democracy. It is essential that residents have all information to make a well-informed decision.

126920.explanation: We want residents to collaborate on generating their own green and affordable energy, for example, through solar panels. We support energy cooperatives that enable this. It is important that everyone can participate, even without a well-filled wallet.

126921.explanation: There is little free space in Westervoort itself to build such distribution centers. We see them in the region already, and there are now enough. Large distribution centers pollute the landscape and add little to the local economy due to the often low-paid migrant labor.

126926.explanation: From 2026, there will be a fireworks ban. We are pleased with this because fireworks cause a lot of nuisance. However, fireworks are an important tradition for many in Westervoort and can foster connection. If there is demand for a fireworks show, a club could apply for a permit.

126929.explanation: Many municipalities have an accessibility fund for physical building adaptations, like ramps, wheelchair access, and such. Westervoort is committed to an inclusive society but does not yet have such a fund. In our opinion, this should be established.

126932.explanation: Each municipality pays a nationally agreed amount per resident to the local broadcaster. Additional funds are not planned. Westervoort has well-read local newspapers and RTV CONNECT, a good regional broadcaster that also covers Westervoort news. We find that valuable.

126937.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. Therefore, we want to invest in safe places. Enforcement is indispensable here. Community enforcement officers can intervene if something goes wrong but mainly maintain a preventive presence. They can also call in neighborhood and youth coaches for neighborhood problems.

126938.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. However, we do not always see more cameras as the right solution. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be easily infringed. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers and especially investment in safe places.

126939.explanation: Women, often with children, must receive safe and protected housing if it’s no longer safe at home, for example due to domestic violence. Westervoort should help these women, and sometimes men, with this. Such shelters are better organized regionally, by the way.

126940.explanation: We find it important that our Westervoort youth, who are often first-time buyers, get support to start on the housing market. We are cautious about the municipality taking on a banking role but want to investigate what role the municipality could play.

126943.explanation: People on social assistance do not choose to be in that situation and need a stable foundation, housing, and trust from the municipality. We are against imposing extra requirements. However, we believe it is important that everyone who can, contributes to society.

126944.explanation: If debt forgiveness of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will get into big trouble. They simply cannot miss that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water, which is why we support debt forgiveness.

126945.explanation: That would be great, but realistically, we aim to help households up to the current threshold of 120% of the social assistance level. This means that anyone who does not have at least an income of 120% of the social assistance level can receive help from the municipality.

126949.explanation: Westervoort residents must receive the care they need. We have confidence in the current healthcare professionals in our village. Where possible, care should be customized. We want to maintain care of the highest possible quality, but with attention to affordability and practical feasibility.

126950.explanation: The cheapest option is not always the best. Excessive cuts in the quality of youth assistance and youth care lead only to extra costs because the care becomes ineffective and young people’s problems worsen.

126952.explanation: The dog tax is an outdated tax with high administrative costs and yields little from dog owners. This makes it an old-fashioned, obsolete tax that we want to abolish as of January 1, 2027.

126955.explanation: Every municipality must house a certain number of status holders. Approximately 10% of freed-up social rental homes are allocated to them. Otherwise, they have no chance of housing and the asylum system becomes further congested. The current system must therefore remain as it is.

126956.explanation: Submitting recyclable waste, such as glass, paper, and plastic/PMD must remain free of charge. However, you must pay for bulky residual waste, which aligns with the principle: the polluter pays. This fee should not be too high because we want to prevent waste from being dumped elsewhere.

126957.explanation: The football clubs and other sports associations in Westervoort must be supported by the municipality because they have an important social function in Westervoort. This also applies, for example, to music associations. This is well established in the new subsidy policy.

126958.explanation: The Netherlands is a water country and every child should learn to swim. Including children whose parents cannot afford swimming lessons. Unfortunately, this is still not arranged fairly. The best solution: swimming lessons at school for every child, with support for families that cannot afford swimming lessons themselves.

126959.explanation: We have the Historical Society in Westervoort, which is supported by the municipality. It is committed to preserving the Liemers accent and culture and can provide lessons in elementary schools.

126961.explanation: There is a severe housing shortage, and high-rise buildings can add many homes. However, the impact of high-rise buildings on the surroundings must be considered, and they should fit the village character of Westervoort. Therefore, we prefer mid-rise buildings over tall tower blocks.

126964.explanation: We consider the current Village Square with shops as the center of Westervoort. It is already car-free and should remain so.

126966.explanation: Now that we are moving towards a merger of the Liemers municipalities, the municipality must keep an eye on the Westervoort community. That means associations must continue to be supported, and the facilities in our village should remain at the same level, such as a municipal service desk.

126967.explanation: Together with Duiven, possibilities are already being explored for an environmental station in Westervoort or at the border with Duiven, so that Westervoort residents no longer have to go to Arnhem.

126916.explanation: Disagree, residents should definitely be asked for their opinion on important matters, and that opinion should weigh heavily and play a role in political considerations. A referendum implies that the residents' opinion must necessarily be followed, which goes too far for us.

126920.explanation: These residents can expect support from the municipality with possible permits or other facilitation, but providing financial support is not seen as a municipal task. This could also lead to unequal treatment compared to other residents.

126926.explanation: This does not appear to be a task for the municipality.

126929.explanation: Accessibility for people with disabilities is certainly important. The municipality can facilitate this but focuses mainly on public spaces and municipal tasks related to this theme. Financially supporting companies is not part of this.

126932.explanation: It should be a regional broadcaster in De Liemers. However, the contribution should be limited. The regional broadcaster should also tap into other sources for funding.

126937.explanation: Careful prioritization within the enforcement work by current BOAs is more important to us than simply appointing more BOAs again.

126938.explanation: And if it must be done, then limit it to the station area.

126939.explanation: Westervoort is too small for that. Suitable facilities are available near Westervoort.

126940.explanation: That does not seem to us to be a task for the municipality.

126943.explanation: The condition is that this must be adjusted to the personal situation.

126944.explanation: The possibilities to waive taxes are not unlimited, though. It cannot be that the cost of waivers is borne solely by property owners through property tax.

126945.explanation: CDA Westervoort sees no reason to adjust the rules on this at present.

126949.explanation: A healthcare provider is expected to provide professional care while considering the wishes of those receiving care. This does not mean that one always has a free choice of caregiver. Availability in the current labor market plays a role here.

126950.explanation: Price is one factor, alongside appropriate deployment, quality, and reliability of the providing organization. Costs in youth care increase significantly every year, and if these increases continue unchecked, society will no longer be able to bear them unless costs are also controlled.

126952.explanation: The municipality bears the costs of cleaning up dog waste and the design and management of dog-walking areas. These costs can be covered by the dog tax.

126955.explanation: Current rules already give priority to status holders; further relaxation would come at the expense of local residents, including Westervoort youth.

126956.explanation: Nothing is free; the costs are recouped through waste disposal fees. The principle here is: 'the polluter pays.'

126957.explanation: Football clubs should primarily operate on membership fees, advertising and sponsorship money, and bar revenues. It has been proven that this is possible. Facility support in managing grounds and buildings is among the possibilities.

126958.explanation: This should be a national task, financed by the central government. If left to municipalities, legal inequality arises in the Netherlands. Everyone in the Netherlands should be able to swim to minimize the risk of drowning.

126961.explanation: CDA Westervoort has no issues with high-rise buildings, but they must fit the environment and not be tower blocks of 10 or more floors.

126964.explanation: Westervoort's center is more than just the shopping center. Making the center car-free is undesirable.

126966.explanation: For the Westervoort community, club life is indispensable and very important. The municipality facilitates this in multiple ways and must continue to do so.

126967.explanation: Westervoort is too small to provide facilities on its own. Moreover, it is more efficient to do this together with other municipalities. This is also the intention.

126916.explanation: They must always be able to provide their opinion and vision. A referendum within a municipality, however, is not binding.

126920.explanation: Yes, for joint energy generation and, where possible, good facilitation by the municipality.

126921.explanation: If there is a need, undertaking is fine. It must not cause infrastructural problems or other issues.

126926.explanation: But then as an event that goes beyond just firing fireworks at midnight.

126929.explanation: Provided these are older existing buildings that have a social function.

126937.explanation: Enforcement is fine but must be used efficiently. Additionally, possibly expand. Issues should be addressed at the source.

126938.explanation: Where dumping occurs, where a sense of security cannot be resolved otherwise, etc., etc.

126939.explanation: Westervoort faces enough issues stemming from being a growth center.

126940.explanation: It's fine if the starters are from the municipality itself. Unless the national government provides funds to the municipality also for those outside Westervoort.

126943.explanation: It is a safety net, so use it where possible. Look where it can be applied.

126944.explanation: More attention to supporting and facilitating budgeting and monitoring.

126945.explanation: It must be a national policy.

126949.explanation: Unless someone decides themselves to enter the waiting list.

126950.explanation: Quality leads to lower costs and especially nearby.

126952.explanation: Outline a clear policy to change citizens' mindset when it comes to cleaning up excrement and facilitate this with waste bins with bags at entrances of centers and parks.

126955.explanation: Join the waiting line like others.

126958.explanation: Then we treat all children equally.

126959.explanation: This can be arranged at home. First, ensure that Dutch language proficiency is up to standard.

126961.explanation: But it must be of good quality and appearance.

126964.explanation: It is impossible within our infrastructure and bad for the shops in our village.

126966.explanation: It must come from society. Facilitating is fine.

126967.explanation: We are working on this in the Liemers, but it must be doable by car.

126916.explanation: D66 Westervoort believes that residents should be actively involved through consultation evenings, participation platforms, and citizen panels so that their voices are heard. Referenda seem attractive but often offer an overly simplistic yes/no choice on very complex issues.

126920.explanation: It is good to encourage residents to generate sustainable energy together. We believe the municipality should primarily provide good information, advice, and remove bureaucratic obstacles, but not through direct financial support from the municipality.

126921.explanation: In Westervoort, we simply have no available land, and the remaining space is urgently needed for housing and a green living environment. Therefore, D66 believes no new distribution centers should be established in the municipality.

126926.explanation: D66 Westervoort wants that old and new year celebrations proceed safely and pleasantly. A central fireworks show can be a nice alternative to private fireworks, as it causes less nuisance, less damage, and more togetherness. However, we need to carefully consider the costs and environmental impact.

126929.explanation: We believe the municipality should primarily provide good information, advice, and remove bureaucratic obstacles, but not through direct financial support from the municipality.

126932.explanation: D66 believes it is important that the municipality financially supports the local broadcasters through subsidies.

126937.explanation: This means D66 does not automatically want more enforcement officers (BOAs) without conditions, but that BOAs should be deployed where residents feel unsafe. Their deployment must contribute to the feeling of safety and tasks where enforcement is useful; the roles of police and BOAs should be distinct.

126938.explanation: D66 supports camera surveillance only when there is a clear necessity: effective, targeted, and proportional, with an end date or evaluation. Privacy is important; the government must not act as “Big Brother.”

126939.explanation: D66 pays attention to tackling domestic violence and sheltering victims, aligning with the concept behind women's shelters/safe homes. It is important that there are enough shelter places for victims of domestic violence and investments are made in protection.

126940.explanation: D66 believes municipal starter loans should be available to help first-time buyers purchase their first home and thus improve their position on the housing market.

126943.explanation: D66 does not support a general mandatory counter-performance for everyone receiving social assistance benefits. D66 believes there should be customization, and the municipality of Westervoort can best assess what each individual needs to participate in society.

126944.explanation: D66 values fair and social policy where support for those on minimum incomes may be necessary. They advocate for targeted aid and customization so that people with low incomes are not overly burdened but are still encouraged to participate in society.

126945.explanation: D66 believes that people with low incomes should be well supported, which may mean raising the income threshold for poverty assistance so more people in need gain access. D66 states that aid must be effective, fair, and targeted.

126949.explanation: D66 supports care that is personal and respectful, taking religion and culture into account, but recognizes the limitation that suitable care providers are not available everywhere, so full freedom of choice is not always feasible.

126950.explanation: D66 wants Westervoort to prioritize quality over price when purchasing youth care.

126952.explanation: It yields less than the administrative costs. That makes this tax inefficient. Moreover, it aligns with policies in our future merged municipalities.

126955.explanation: D66 Westervoort believes the municipality should continue supporting status holders with residence permits in finding social rental housing, aiming for about 10% of the annually available social rental homes in Westervoort to be allocated for this purpose.

126956.explanation: D66 believes it should be as easy as possible for residents to sustainably dispose of waste at the recycling center and that service must be good, but the principle ‘the polluter pays’ should be applied.

126957.explanation: D66 believes there should be no distinction between soccer clubs and other sports clubs, and thus they should not receive extra funds compared to other associations. Sports clubs in Westervoort already receive subsidies from the municipality.

126958.explanation: D66 Westervoort believes all children should have the opportunity to learn to swim, regardless of their parents' income. Therefore, children with a Gelrepas should be able to take diploma swimming lessons for free. Parents can decide where their children take the lessons.

126959.explanation: Education should primarily focus on equal opportunities and basic skills such as language, arithmetic, and digital skills. D66 Westervoort values our local culture but does not believe that dialect education should be a mandatory part of all primary schools.

126961.explanation: D66 Westervoort believes that taller residential buildings can be allowed in the municipality as long as they do not exceed about five stories, as this contributes to the housing supply without disturbing the character of the municipality.

126964.explanation: D66 Westervoort believes the center should primarily be safe, accessible, and livable for everyone. Our center is already nearly completely car-free.

126966.explanation: A strong community life is essential for our society. D66 believes Westervoort should continue investing in local clubs and initiatives so that residents of all ages can participate, talents can develop, and social bonds in our village can further grow.

126967.explanation: Westervoort is too small for its own waste disposal facility. D66 sees added value in cooperation with Arnhem and/or the municipalities of the Liemers region, so that residents can dispose of their waste efficiently and sustainably without duplicating efforts or incurring high costs for the municipality of Westervoort.

126916.explanation: Important matters deserve a broad majority; then a consultative referendum can be held. Not all topics are suitable for a referendum.

126920.explanation: Sustainability should be possible for everyone who wants it.

126921.explanation: In Westervoort there is no room for large distribution centers, unlike neighboring municipalities such as Duiven.

126926.explanation: After the national fireworks ban is implemented, the municipality must investigate whether this desire exists within Westervoort.

126929.explanation: The municipality does not need to provide financial support, but it can offer advice.

126932.explanation: Westervoort has no local broadcaster, so extra support is unnecessary. Local newspapers are considered very important by Wij Westervoort.

126937.explanation: The deployment of enforcement officers (BOAs) is important for enforcement, but coaching and prevention are even more crucial; therefore, Wij Westervoort focuses on more street coaches.

126938.explanation: More camera surveillance may be installed at specific locations such as the station.

126939.explanation: Westervoort lacks the resources to support a women's shelter. Additionally, this is a regional policy, not a municipal one.

126940.explanation: Wij Westervoort wants the budget for providing starter loans to be increased so that starters from Westervoort have a chance to remain living in Westervoort.

126943.explanation: When physically and mentally possible, we want to encourage people to participate in society in this way.

126944.explanation: Debt forgiveness is a method to prevent or reduce debt problems. It must be critically examined whether debt forgiveness is the right solution for each individual case.

126945.explanation: Westervoort applies the 120% social assistance norm, as many other municipalities do. Wij Westervoort supports this norm.

126949.explanation: Wij Westervoort believes that people should be allowed to choose an organization that fits their needs, provided it is available in the region.

126950.explanation: Quality of care is important. Westervoort faces challenges with its youth and must therefore focus on qualitative youth care rather than fulfilling its statutory obligations on a low budget.

126952.explanation: Wij Westervoort advocates abolishing the dog tax as of January 2027.

126955.explanation: Wij Westervoort recognizes that there are already few opportunities for young people and starters from Westervoort to continue living in Westervoort; this should be the priority.

126956.explanation: A fee must be paid for disposing waste at the waste disposal facility. Wij Westervoort does not want to increase this fee but believes the polluter should pay.

126957.explanation: Sport is important for the well-being of residents. Besides health benefits, sport has an important social function. Wij Westervoort supports the subsidy policy established in 2025.

126958.explanation: Now that Westervoort no longer has its own swimming pool, school swimming is difficult and costly to organize. Wij Westervoort prefers to spend that money elsewhere.

126959.explanation: Dialects do not need to receive attention in schools. However, Wij Westervoort welcomes initiatives that honor the dialect.

126961.explanation: Westervoort has very limited space for new homes. This makes high-rise buildings in certain locations inevitable to address the housing shortage.

126964.explanation: The village square is car-free, and Wij Westervoort wants to keep it that way.

126966.explanation: The community life is an important factor in Westervoort that increases mutual connections. The municipality should facilitate and stimulate community life more.

126967.explanation: The waste disposal facilities in the surrounding municipalities are easily accessible. Westervoort itself has no space or facilities.

statements.title: Westervoort should do more for the village's community life.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Westervoort should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Residents of Westervoort should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Westervoort moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Westervoort.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
Westervoort should establish a shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should enable all primary schools to include lessons on the Kleverland dialect.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling centre should remain free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Westervoort should give more money to the local football clubs.
High-rise residential buildings should be allowed in Westervoort.
The center of Westervoort should be made car-free.
Westervoort should have its own recycling center where residents can dispose of their waste.

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

titleParts.text: krijgen waar inwoners hun afval kwijt kunnen.
